title: Thai Green Curry
method: Cut chicken thighs into long thin slices. Mix with 1 tbsp curry paste and season with salt
Heat 1 tbsp oil in a pan, add chicken and fry for 5 mins until cooked through. Transfer cooked chicken to a plate
Add remaining oil to pan and fry onions for 10 mins until softened. Transfer to plate

Scoop top third (super creamy top part) of both cans of coconut milk into pan. Heat over medium low heat and slowly bring to a simmer until the water and fat separate. Add curry paste and stir fry for 3 mins
Add onions back into pan followed by the rest of the coconut milk and fish sauce. Stir the mixture and bring to a boil
Add chicken back into pan, bring to boil then reduce heat to a simmer
Add sugarsnap peas into curry, stir for 1 minute then remove pan from heat. Serve with rice
notes: Use Mae Ploy curry paste
Add bamboo shoots with the sugarsnap peas
Can use galangal instead of ginger

title: Korean Beef
method: Melt 1 tbsp butter in pan, add garlic and stir fry until fragrant
Add minced beef and cook until browned
In a separate cup mix together brown sugar, soy sauce, and gochujang
Add sauce and ginger to beef, and stir until coated. Serve with rice
notes: Serve with boiled broccoli - 2 broccoli for a week's worth

title: Crispy Lemon Chicken
ingredients: 600g chicken breast fillets, cut into 2cm pieces
2 tbsp sunflower or vegetable oil, for frying
2 spring onions, finely sliced, to serve
method: Whisk 2 tsp cornflour with lemon zest and juice until smooth; add honey, soy, sesame seeds, and sesame oil. Set aside.
Combine 2 tbsp cornflour, plain flour, baking powder, 1/4 tsp salt, pepper, egg, and 4 tbsp water in a bowl.
Toss chicken in the batter.
Heat oil and fry chicken in batches for 6 mins until golden; drain on kitchen paper.
Return all chicken to the pan over high heat, pour in the sauce and toss until thickened and clinging to the chicken.
Scatter with spring onions and serve.
notes: Serve with noodles if desired.
Nutritional info: 280 kcal per serving (serves 6).

title: Gochujang Glazed Chicken
ingredients: 3 green onions, sliced (separate whites and greens)
method: Combine gochujang, gochugaru, soy sauce, oyster sauce, bouillon, honey, mirin, water, and cornstarch in a bowl.
Pat chicken dry, season with salt/pepper, and coat with potato starch.
Sear chicken skin-side down in oil for 4 mins; flip and cook 4 more mins until done. Remove chicken.
In the same pan, sauté garlic, ginger, and white parts of green onions until fragrant.
Add sauce to the pan and bring to a boil until thickened.
Add chicken back to the pan and toss for 1-2 minutes until coated.
notes: Serve over rice with optional asparagus stir-fry and soft-boiled eggs.
Potato starch coating is optional but helps the sauce stick better.

title: One Hour Bread
ingredients: 2 tbsp olive oil (plus more for brushing)
3/4 tsp kosher salt (plus more for topping)
method: Preheat oven to 400°F.
Stir yeast, brown sugar, and warm water; let sit for 5 mins to bloom.
Add flour, oil, salt, and herbs. Stir until flour is incorporated and pulls from the bowl.
Form into a ball and let proof in a warm place for 20 mins.
Place on a baking sheet, cut 3-4 slashes on top, and brush with oil/salt.
Bake for 30-35 mins until golden brown.
notes: For a darker crust, you can brush with milk before baking.
Proofing in a warm microwave with a cup of boiling water helps the speed.

title: Jollof Rice
ingredients: Cayenne pepper and red pepper flakes
method: Brown chicken pieces in a pot and set aside.
Sauté onions until translucent, then add tomato paste and fry for several minutes.
Add the blended pepper/tomato mixture and cook down until reduced.
Stir in spices, stock, and rice.
Bring to a boil, then cover and simmer on low heat until rice is tender.
Fold in vegetables like carrots and green beans near the end of cooking.
notes: Make sure to turn heat to high to bring to a simmer after adding sauce to rice.
Parboiled rice is essential for the traditional texture to prevent mushiness.

title: One-Pot Garlic Chicken
method: Coat chicken in flour and season.
Fry chicken in oil for 1-2 mins per side until golden; remove from pan.
Add butter and whole peeled garlic cloves to the pan; cook 5 mins until golden.
Return chicken to pan, pour in stock, and simmer for 10 mins.
Stir in cream and cheese; simmer for 5 mins until sauce thickens.
Scatter with parsley and serve.
notes: Great served with rice or jacket potatoes.
Thighs are recommended as they are harder to overcook than breasts.

title: Oven Pork Chops
method: Preheat oven to 220C/430F.
Toss potatoes in oil/salt/pepper on a tray and bake for 15 mins.
Mix ketchup, soy, sugar, oil, garlic, vinegar, and Worcestershire sauce for the rub.
Slather pork chops with the rub.
Add pork to the tray with potatoes and bake for 15 mins.
Flip pork, brush with remaining sauce, and broil/grill for 5-10 mins until caramelised.
notes: Ensure potatoes are no larger than 3cm to cook in time.
Use bone-in chops for the best flavor and juiciness.

title: Matcha Shokupan
method: Make tangzhong: whisk 15g flour, matcha, 20ml milk, and 20ml water over low heat into a paste. Cool.
Activate yeast in warm milk and sugar.
Mix tangzhong, flour, salt, egg, and yeast mixture in a stand mixer.
Knead until smooth, then incorporate butter pieces. Let rise for 60-90 mins.
Divide into 3, roll into rectangles, coil like snails, and place in a Pullman pan.
Rise until near top of pan, then bake at 350F for 30-35 mins.
notes: A Pullman loaf pan is required for the signature square shape.
Let cool completely before slicing to set the texture.

title: Oven Chicken Thighs
method: Preheat oven to 400°F.
Season chicken thighs with salt and pepper.
Whisk together olive oil, both mustards, honey, garlic, and red pepper flakes.
Coat chicken thoroughly in the sauce.
Place chicken in a baking dish and bake for 30-35 minutes until the skin is crispy and internal temp is 165°F.
notes: For extra crispy skin, you can broil for the last 2-3 minutes.
Bone-in thighs provide much more flavor than boneless.

title: Air Fryer Doughnuts
method: Mix dry ingredients; add milk, egg, yeast, and butter.
Knead for 10-15 mins (or 3-5 in mixer) until smooth. Let rise for 2 hours.
Roll dough to 1-inch thickness and cut into circles with a hole.
Let shaped doughnuts rise for 45 mins.
Preheat air fryer to 180°C. Grease basket with butter.
Air fry for 4 minutes until golden, then coat in cinnamon sugar while warm.
notes: Do not roll the dough too thin or they will be flat.
Best eaten fresh on the day they are made.

title: Crispy Pork Belly (Siu Yuk)
method: Prick hundreds of holes in the skin with a skewer (do not pierce the meat).
Rub wine and spices onto the meat side only. Refrigerate uncovered for 12-24 hours to dry the skin.
Preheat oven to 180C/350F. Wrap pork in foil so only the skin is exposed.
Brush skin with vinegar and cover with a thick layer of rock salt. Roast for 60 mins.
Remove salt and foil. Broil/grill for 20-25 mins until skin is puffed and golden.
notes: Rock salt prevents the pork from getting too salty and is easy to remove.
Avoid piercing into the fat/meat when pricking holes to prevent juices from ruining the crackling.

title: Pork Belly Sandwiches
method: Cut the skin off the pork belly and pat dry with kitchen roll
Rub belly with olive oil, then season with dry rub ingredients
Wrap pork belly with foil
Air fry for 50 minutes at 190C - Tighten foil at 30 minutes
Remove from air fryer, and allow to rest for 15 minutes
Make the sauce - mix mustard, honey, brown sugar and paprika into saucepan and bring to simmer, whisking until dissolved
Cube the pork and add to the sauce
notes: Pork can be baked in oven for 1 hour at 140C then 1.5 hours at 240C

title: One Hour Bread
method: Melt 1 tbsp butter in pan, add garlic and stir fry until fragrant
Add minced beef and cook until browned
In a separate cup mix together brown sugar, soy sauce, and gochujang
Add sauce and ginger to beef, and stir until coated. Serve with rice
notes: Serve with boiled broccoli - 2 broccoli for a week's worth

method: Melt 1 tbsp butter in pan, add garlic and stir fry until fragrant
Add minced beef and cook until browned
In a separate cup mix together brown sugar, soy sauce, and gochujang
Add sauce and ginger to beef, and stir until coated. Serve with rice

method: Melt 1 tbsp butter in pan, add garlic and stir fry until fragrant
Add minced beef and cook until browned

Add sauce and ginger to beef, and stir until coated. Serve with rice

Add sauce and ginger to beef, and stir until coated. Serve with rice
notes: Serve with boiled broccoli - 2 broccoli for a week's worth

title: Cream Corn Chicken
ingredients: ½ can of water (using the cream corn can)
method: Cut chicken thighs into small cubes.
Season the cubed chicken with salt and garlic powder. Leave for 15 minutes.
Fry the chicken until golden brown.
Decrease the heat and add in the 2 cans of cream corn plus half a can of water (using one of the corn cans to measure).
Increase heat until the mixture comes to a boil.
Stir in thickening granules (or your preferred thickener) until the sauce reaches the desired consistency.
notes: Serve with rice.

title: Lasagne
ingredients: 9-12 lasagne sheets (depending on the size of your baking dish)
method: In a large pan, heat the olive oil over a low heat. Fry chopped garlic until softened.
Add the mince and fry on a medium heat until golden.
Stir in chopped tomatoes and stock. Simmer for 15 mins, or until the liquid has reduced.
Meanwhile, make the white sauce. Melt the butter in a small saucepan over a low heat and add the flour. Whisk until combined and cook on low for 1-2 mins.
Remove from the heat and gradually whisk in the milk until you have a loose sauce. Season. Return to a gentle heat and whisk constantly until the sauce thickens.
Preheat the oven to gas 6, 200°C, fan 180°C.
Layer up the lasagne in a baking dish, starting with a third each of the meat, then the pasta, then the white sauce. Repeat twice.
Bake in the oven for 40-45 mins, until piping hot and crisp and bubbling on top. Serve immediately.
notes: Budget swap: Use frozen beef and pork mince mix instead of fresh mince.
Freezing: Once cooled, freeze in an airtight container for 1-3 months. Defrost in fridge overnight before reheating until piping hot.

title: Hawaii Butter Mochi
ingredients: 16 oz box mochiko (or 454g glutinous rice flour)
method: Preheat oven to 176C (350F).
Mix dry ingredients (mochiko, sugar, salt, baking powder).
Add in wet ingredients (coconut milk, evaporated milk, melted butter, eggs, extract) and mix until well combined.
Scrape down the sides of the bowl to check for any lumps of mochiko that did not get incorporated.
Pour into a greased 9x13" (22x33cm) baking pan, sprinkle shredded coconut on top (if using).
Bake for an hour, or until a golden brown crust has formed on top and the middle is set. (If using a metal pan, check at 45 minutes).
Let cool and slice into rectangles
notes: Leftovers can keep in an airtight container at room temperature for a few days.
You can freeze individual pieces and defrost at room temperature or reheat in the microwave.
You can use glutinous rice flour instead of Mochiko
